title: /k test
summary_text: Ran `bash tests/test-codex-kernel-prompt.sh` and it passed. That static check verifies the repo-local `/k` alias and `/kernel` prompt contract via [tests/test-codex-kernel-prompt.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-feed-runner/tests/test-codex-kernel-prompt.sh) and [scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-feed-runner/scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh). I did not run the fresh-session smoke path (`RUN_CODEX_KERNEL_SMOKE=1 ...`), because that requires a fresh Codex session and live bootstrap behavior. One caveat: when I tried to honor `/k` literally, background Kernel lanes hit a `GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark` usage limit, so this session could not sustain a compliant 6-lane Kernel bootstrap. The local static test itself still passed.
